title: NOC 13111 – Legal Administrative Assistants | GTR Immigration
description: Legal Administrative Assistants support law firms, courts, corporate legal departments, and government agencies across Canada. Classified under NOC 13111, this occupation continues to experience steady hiring nationwide and offers practical immigration opportunities through skilled worker programs, Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs), and employer-supported work permits.

classification.teer: 3 – Occupations usually require a college diploma or apprenticeship training of less than two years; or more than six months of on-the-job training
classification.minor_group: 1311 – Office administrative assistants – general, legal, and medical

common_job_titles: Administrative assistant – legal Corporate law legal assistant Legal assistant Legal assistant – criminal law Legal secretary Litigation legal assistant Litigation secretary Real estate secretary
duties: Prepare and type correspondence and legal documents such as deeds, wills, affidavits, and briefs
Proofread legal materials for procedural accuracy and grammar
Schedule appointments, meetings, and court-related conferences
Maintain filing systems and manage confidential legal records
Supervise or train junior staff in procedures and software
Attend court or meetings to take notes and minutes
Perform general office tasks, including preparing financial statements
employment_requirements: One- or two-year college programs in legal administration or secretarial studies
job_outlook: From 2022 to 2031, Canada is expected to create 18,000 new positions for Legal Administrative Assistants, while 18,400 job seekers are projected—making careful province selection and immigration planning important for overseas applicants.

additional_information: Legal Administrative Assistants work in private law firms, corporate legal teams, courts, and public-sector legal offices.

Continued post-pandemic hiring in legal servicesStable clerical and litigation-support rolesEligibility for Express Entry and Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs)Employer-sponsored work permit optionsAnnual earnings commonly ranging from $32,800 to $71,900 CAD
